result.markdown:
---
title: "Normalization & Feature Selection"
subtitle: "Step 3: Data normalization and HVG selection"
sidebar: scrnaseq
---

## Overview

Normalization removes technical variation while preserving biological signal. Feature selection identifies genes with high cell-to-cell variability for downstream analysis.

## Standard Normalization (LogNormalize)


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
library(Seurat)

# Log-normalize data
seurat_obj <- NormalizeData(seurat_obj, 
                            normalization.method = "LogNormalize",
                            scale.factor = 10000)

# Find variable features
seurat_obj <- FindVariableFeatures(seurat_obj,
                                   selection.method = "vst",
                                   nfeatures = 2000)

# Visualize variable features
top10 <- head(VariableFeatures(seurat_obj), 10)
plot1 <- VariableFeaturePlot(seurat_obj)
plot2 <- LabelPoints(plot = plot1, points = top10, repel = TRUE)
plot2
```
:::


## SCTransform (Recommended)

SCTransform uses regularized negative binomial regression and typically performs better than log-normalization.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
# SCTransform normalization
seurat_obj <- SCTransform(seurat_obj, 
                          vars.to.regress = "percent.mt",
                          verbose = FALSE)
```
:::


::: {.callout-tip}
## SCTransform v2

For the latest version with improved performance:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
seurat_obj <- SCTransform(seurat_obj, 
                          vars.to.regress = "percent.mt",
                          vst.flavor = "v2",
                          verbose = FALSE)
```
:::

:::

## Cell Cycle Scoring

Cell cycle effects can dominate clustering. Score and optionally regress cell cycle genes.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
# Load cell cycle genes
s.genes <- cc.genes.updated.2019$s.genes
g2m.genes <- cc.genes.updated.2019$g2m.genes

# Score cell cycle
seurat_obj <- CellCycleScoring(seurat_obj,
                               s.features = s.genes,
                               g2m.features = g2m.genes,
                               set.ident = FALSE)

# View distribution
table(seurat_obj$Phase)

# Visualize
VlnPlot(seurat_obj, features = c("S.Score", "G2M.Score"), group.by = "Phase")
```
:::


### Regressing Cell Cycle (Optional)


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Option 1: Regress out all cell cycle effects
seurat_obj <- SCTransform(seurat_obj, 
                          vars.to.regress = c("percent.mt", "S.Score", "G2M.Score"),
                          verbose = FALSE)

# Option 2: Regress difference between S and G2M (preserves cycling vs non-cycling)
seurat_obj$CC.Difference <- seurat_obj$S.Score - seurat_obj$G2M.Score
seurat_obj <- SCTransform(seurat_obj,
                          vars.to.regress = c("percent.mt", "CC.Difference"),
                          verbose = FALSE)
```
:::


## Scaling Data

For log-normalized data, scale before PCA:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Scale data (for LogNormalize workflow)
all.genes <- rownames(seurat_obj)
seurat_obj <- ScaleData(seurat_obj, features = all.genes)

# Or scale with regression
seurat_obj <- ScaleData(seurat_obj, 
                        vars.to.regress = c("percent.mt", "nCount_RNA"))
```
:::


::: {.callout-note}
## SCTransform Note

When using SCTransform, scaling is included in the workflow. Do not run `ScaleData()` separately.
:::

## Examining Variable Features


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Get top variable features
top_hvg <- head(VariableFeatures(seurat_obj), 20)

# Expression heatmap of top HVGs
DoHeatmap(seurat_obj, features = top_hvg, cells = 500)
```
:::


## Save Checkpoint


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Save normalized object
saveRDS(seurat_obj, "seurat_normalized.rds")
```
:::


## Next Steps

With normalized data, proceed to [Clustering](04-clustering.qmd).
